data.title: VivaMed partners with Syngene to advance AI-driven therapeutic programs, leveraging global validation infrastructure.
data.description: A clinical-stage pharmaceutical company, VivaMed BioPharma, based in Scottsdale, Arizona, has partnered with Syngene International, a global contract research, development, and manufacturing organization. This partnership aims to utilize Artificial Intelligence to discover and advance repurposed and improved therapeutic assets. VivaMed's collaboration with Syngene enhances its validation infrastructure, demonstrating a commitment to delivering scientifically validated products. This strategic alliance signifies a significant step in advancing VivaMed's mission and growth trajectory.

data.title: Liquibase Secure 5.1 expands into Snowflake, enhancing security and database coverage for compliance and AI, closing gaps.
data.description: In Austin, Texas, Liquibase Secure 5.1 has recently been launched to address a crucial need in data platform security, compliance, and AI preparedness. This latest version not only fills a critical gap but also broadens its database platform coverage.

data.title: Triton Digital's 2025 U.S. Podcast Report: Podcasting reaches 53% of U.S. pop monthly, showing industry's growth.
data.description: In New York, Triton Digital®, a global technology and services provider for digital audio, podcast, and broadcast radio industries, unveiled its 2025 U.S. Podcast Report. The report provides insights into podcast listening habits in the U.S., covering devices, platforms, genres, and demographics. With podcast consumption now reaching 53% of the U.S. population monthly, this marks the first time podcasting has surpassed the halfway mark, highlighting its increasing impact.

data.title: YETI reported 7% sales growth in Q4 2025; 25% in international sales. Returned $300M to shareholders. Full-year 2025 results: 2% sales growth, $254.7M operating cash flow, $212.1M free cash flow. Plans 6-8% sales growth, $2.77-$2.83 adjusted EPS in 2026.
data.description: YETI Holdings, Inc., a designer and retailer of outdoor products, reported its financial results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year ended January 3, 2026. In the fourth quarter of 2025, net sales increased 7%, international net sales grew by 25%, and drinkware net sales increased by 6%. YETI returned nearly $300 million to shareholders through share repurchases in 2025.For the full year 2025, net sales increased by 2%, with international net sales growing by 16%. Operating cash flow was $254.7 million, and free cash flow was $212.1 million. YETI repurchased 8.2 million shares for $297.6 million during the year.YETI's President and CEO highlighted the company's strong performance during Q4 and expressed confidence in the outlook for 2026, expecting sales growth between 6% and 8%. Adjusted net income per diluted share is projected to increase by 12% to 14%, with capital expenditures between $60 million and $70 million and free cash flow between $200 million and $225 million.The company aims to drive growth through innovation, brand strength, international expansion, and a global product engine. YETI continues to focus on enhancing profitability, investing in growth, and delivering value to shareholders. Their long-term plan targets high-single to low-double digit growth, backed by a diverse innovation pipeline and strategic financial strength.

data.title: BSV Association announces BSV listing on LCX, Europe's compliant exchange, aligning with MiCA.
data.description: On February 19, 2026, in ZUG, Switzerland, the BSV Association revealed that BSV will now be available for trading on LCX, a prominent European cryptocurrency exchange and token-to-stablecoin platform known for its regulatory compliance. This listing on LCX is a major achievement for the BSV Association, as it expands the reach of BSV within the cryptocurrency market and offers new trading opportunities for investors.

data.title: Lemonade (NYSE: LMND) beats Q4 earnings with EPS of $-0.29 (27.5% above estimate) and revenue up $79.30 million from last year.
data.description: Lemonade (NYSE: LMND) announced its Q4 earnings on Thursday, February 19, 2026, at 06:01 AM. The key highlights include the company surpassing estimated earnings by 27.5%, with an EPS of $-0.29 compared to the expected $-0.4. Revenue showed a significant increase of $79.30 million from the previous year. In past earnings, Lemonade exceeded EPS projections by $0.2. For further details, the full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Lemonade (LMND) Q4 earnings beat estimates by 27.5%, EPS $-0.29 vs. $-0.4. Revenue increased $79.30 million YoY.
data.description: Lemonade (NYSE: LMND) disclosed its Q4 financial results on February 19, 2026, at 06:01 AM. The report highlights the following key points for investors to take note of. Lemonade exceeded projected earnings by a substantial 27.5%, with an EPS of $-0.29 compared to the estimated $-0.40. Revenue witnessed a significant increase of $79.30 million from the corresponding period in the previous year. In previous earnings, the company outperformed expectations on EPS. For further details, the complete article can be accessed on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Day Out Snacks adds plant-based protein balls in 75 new stores like Dierbergs Markets and Costco Bay Area locations.
data.description: A company called Day Out Snacks has introduced plant-based protein balls to Dierbergs Markets and Costco Bay Area locations. On Feb. 19, 2026, the company disclosed its expansion into 75 additional retail locations. The vegan protein snacks, known for their dessert-inspired flavors, are now available in a total of 75 storefronts.

data.title: 70% of global ransomware attacks target English-speaking countries, with 40% targeting US-based entities.
data.description: On February 19, 2026, a report from Nozomi Networks Labs on OT & IoT Security revealed that 70% of ransomware attacks worldwide are focusing on English-speaking nations. In the latter half of last year, 40% of such attacks were aimed at entities in the United States.

data.title: CEA offers three ways for Arizona taxpayers to support foster K-12 students with quality education.
data.description: On February 19, 2026, in Phoenix, Catholic Education Arizona (CEA) introduced three significant opportunities for Arizona taxpayers and businesses to assist underserved, disabled foster students in grades K-12. This initiative aims to offer these students access to a quality, values-driven education, benefiting from lifelong advantages. Through these programs, Arizona residents and businesses can make a positive impact on the lives of these students.

data.title: BT expands with 35 lawyers in public finance and infrastructure, opening 3 new offices in 10 markets. 25 partners included.
data.description: Around 50 attorneys and business experts are set to join, expanding Barnes & Thornburg's highly regarded teams in 10 different regions, with new locations opening in Baltimore, Denver, and Phoenix. The addition includes 35 lawyers, with 25 of them being partners. The development is part of the company's expansion strategy and marks a significant growth for the firm in Philadelphia and Washington.

data.title: Netchex achieves 98% customer satisfaction score, emphasizing dedication to excellent service.
data.description: A payroll and HCM provider from Covington, Louisiana, made a grand statement by showcasing their hometown service values on a Times Square billboard on February 19, 2026. Netchex, known for offering top-notch payroll and HCM solutions, achieved an impressive 98% Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) score.

data.title: YETI reports solid results for Q4 2025: Net Sales up 7%, Intl Net Sales up 25%, and $300M returned to shareholders in share repurchases. Full year 2026 outlook provided.
data.description: In the fourth quarter, there was a 7% rise in net sales and a 5% growth in adjusted net sales. International net sales surged by 25%, while drinkware net sales saw a 6% increase during this period. In 2025, the company returned almost $300 million to shareholders through share repurchases.

data.title: Canadian Tire Corp. (TSX: CTC) reports strong Q4 2025 with 4.2% sales growth and Q4 Retail Revenue up 8.8%. FY 2025 Diluted EPS up 18.6% at $13.77. Progress in True North strategy, including stronger connection with customers and higher retail performance. Retail Sales up 8.9%, Retail Revenue up 8.8% in Q4. Normalized IBT up 32.5% to $349.6M in Q4. Continuing to leverage margin management tools and streamline organization for improved retail operational performance. Triangle Rewards loyalty program grows to 9.8M members. Operating model efficiency in place, with savings of $30M reflected in Q4. Total capital expenditures in 2025 at $630.5M. Quarterly dividend declared at $1.80 per share. Share repurchased $442.4M worth of Class A Non-Voting Shares in 2025. Non-GAAP measures and supplementary financial details provided. Full details available on Benzinga.com.
data.description: Canadian Tire Corporation, Limited (TSX: CTC) reported strong results for Q4 and full-year 2025, with consolidated comparable sales up 4.2% and 4.1% respectively. Q4 retail revenue growth was 8.8%, reaching $4,551.1 million, and normalized diluted EPS for continuing operations saw a significant increase of 38.0% in Q4 at $4.47. FY normalized diluted EPS also rose by 18.6% to $13.77. The company's True North transformational growth strategy led to improvements in retail operational performance, with Retail IBT increasing by 32.5% to $349.6 million in Q4. Retail sales, excluding Petroleum, were up 10.7% for FY 2025. Canadian Tire's refreshed stores and enhanced Triangle Rewards loyalty program contributed to the growth, and the company's True North restructuring yielded approximately $30 million in operating expense savings in Q4, supporting future investments in AI deployment. The Board declared a dividend of $1.80 per share for June 1, 2026, and reported share repurchases totaling $442.4 million during FY 2025. The Company's Retail segment showed strong growth, with Retail sales reaching $5,860.7 million in Q4, and CTR, SportChek, and Mark's banners all experiencing notable sales and comparable sales growth. Financial Services segment IBT was $79.2 million in Q4, contributing to the overall positive performance in 2025. Helly Hansen's results were separated as discontinued operations, with Capital Expenditures for 2025 totaling $630.5 million. The Company aims to continue balancing savings with investments to support growth and advance the True North strategy in the coming years.

data.title: Six Flags reports 2025 Q4 and full year results. Key numbers not provided in the summary.
data.description: In Charlotte, North Carolina, Six Flags Entertainment Corporation has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2025. The company reported its earnings and performance during this period, indicating key figures for investors and stakeholders to review.

data.title: MTY Group reports Q4 2025 results: Net income up, $32.1M; Adjusted EBITDA $87.7M; 19 net new store openings. System sales $1.41B.
data.description: MTY Food Group Inc. reported financial results for Q4 of fiscal 2025, with segment profits up by 48% to $87.3 million, thanks to a one-time catch up of gift card breakage income. Net income reached $32.1 million, compared to a loss of $(55.3) million in Q4-24. Cash flows from operations were $46.2 million, increased by $2.5 million from Q4-24. Long-term debt repayments were $13.8 million for the quarter and $69.9 million since Q4-24. Normalized adjusted EBITDA increased 48% to $87.7 million. Net store openings were 19, ending the year with 7,080 locations. System sales increased to $1.4 billion for the quarter due to a 53rd week, but excluding that, sales decreased by 1%. Same-store sales decreased by 1.7%. Adjusted earnings per share were $1.88. The network had 7,080 locations, with 85 new openings and 66 closures in Q4. System sales increased by 3% but decreased by 2% excluding the 53rd week. Digital sales increased by 1%. Revenue reached $305.4 million, and net income attributable to owners was $32.1 million. Normalized adjusted EBITDA increased to $87.7 million in Q4-25.
